name: Four Seasons Resort The Nam Hai

content.verdict: Four Seasons Resort The Nam Hai is consistently ranked among Asia's finest beach resorts — 100 private pool villas spread across three gentle terraces descending to Ha My Beach near Hoi An, Vietnam's most romantic town. The resort's cooking school brings Vietnamese culinary traditions to honeymooners in a setting of extraordinary beauty, while the ancient lantern-lit streets of Hoi An are just 20 minutes away. Central Vietnam's cultural richness and this resort's world-class facilities create an ideal combination.
content.best_room: The Beach Villas are the ultimate Nam Hai experience — private pool villas sitting directly on the South China Sea, with direct beach access and a sense of complete seclusion despite the resort's 100-villa scale. The larger Beach Villas provide two bedrooms for couples who want space to spread out. Hillside Pool Villas offer the best elevated views over the resort's three infinity pools and the sea, often at a more accessible price point. Every villa includes a private plunge pool.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Arrival & Ha My Beach
itinerary_7_nights.description: Fly into Da Nang International Airport and transfer 30 minutes south to the resort. Your villa butler welcomes you with Vietnamese spring rolls and coconut water as the South China Sea glitters beyond the pool. The afternoon is for the beach: Ha My's 1.5km of white sand fronts the resort exclusively, with sunbeds, cold towels, and fresh coconuts on demand.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Hoi An Ancient Town
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive 20 minutes to the UNESCO World Heritage town of Hoi An — a perfectly preserved 15th-century trading port where Japanese merchant houses, Chinese assembly halls, and Vietnamese tube houses coexist on lantern-hung streets. Morning market visit, tailoring appointment (suits and ao dais made in 24 hours), and lunch at Bale Well for bánh mì and white rose dumplings.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Cooking School & Boat Market
itinerary_7_nights.description: The Nam Hai Cooking School is one of Southeast Asia's finest. A morning begins at the Hoi An river market selecting fresh ingredients by boat before returning to the resort kitchen to learn six Vietnamese classics including pho and bánh xèo sizzling pancakes. Cook together, then eat what you made at the resort's poolside restaurant.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Spa Day & My Son Sanctuary
itinerary_7_nights.description: Morning at the Heart of The Earth Spa — a treatment using Vietnamese ingredients: green rice scrubs, turmeric wraps, and lemongrass massage oils in an open-air sala overlooking tropical gardens. Afternoon excursion to My Son Hindu sanctuary — 4th-century Cham towers in a jungle valley, Vietnam's answer to Angkor.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Sunrise Beach & Cycle Tour
itinerary_7_nights.description: A private sunrise yoga session on the beach at 6am — soft light, empty sand, the resort to yourselves before other guests stir. Later, cycle through the Hoi An countryside on rented bicycles: rice paddy paths, village markets, and an authentic lunch at a farming family's home table.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Marble Mountains & Da Nang
itinerary_7_nights.description: Day trip to Da Nang: the Dragon Bridge (which breathes fire on weekend evenings), the Marble Mountains Buddhist sanctuaries carved into natural rock, and the Museum of Cham Sculpture. Return via China Beach for a sunset swim before the resort's candlelit dinner on your villa deck.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Final Pool Day & Departure
itinerary_7_nights.description: Last morning in the private pool as the Ha My tide comes in. The resort breakfast — a vast spread of Vietnamese and international dishes under the pavilion — is a worthy final act. Transfer to Da Nang for the international connection, suitcases heavier with tailored clothing and spice packets from the Hoi An market.

caveats: Ha My Beach is subject to seasonal jellyfish presence and rip currents in certain months — check conditions with the resort's beach team before swimming.
Central Vietnam's typhoon season runs from October to December, with September and November particularly prone to heavy rain; consider February-August for best weather.
Hoi An and Da Nang in high season (July-August) are extremely popular with domestic tourists; some sites can be crowded, especially on weekends.

faqs.question: Good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: Four Seasons Nam Hai is exceptional for honeymooners combining beach luxury with cultural immersion. Hoi An's romantic streets, the cooking school, and 100 private pool villas create a perfect balance of relaxation and adventure. The resort is consistently cited in top honeymoon destination lists across Asia.

faqs.question: Best time to visit?
faqs.answer: February through August offers the best weather for central Vietnam. March to May is particularly lovely — warm, dry, and uncrowded. June and July are hot but excellent for swimming. November to January is shoulder season with occasional rain but fewer visitors and lower rates. Avoid October which has the highest typhoon risk.

faqs.question: Book in advance?
faqs.answer: Beach Villas book out 4 to 6 months ahead for peak season. The resort is popular year-round given Hoi An's international appeal. Mention your honeymoon at reservation for complimentary villa décor and a dedicated butler introduction.

faqs.question: Adults-only?
faqs.answer: No — Four Seasons Nam Hai is family-friendly with a kids' club and family villa categories. The beach villa area and adult pools provide spaces of genuine quiet for honeymooners, and the resort's scale means families and couples coexist comfortably.

faqs.question: Best room type?
faqs.answer: Beach Villas are the most romantic — private pools with direct beach access and the sound of the South China Sea from the villa. Hillside Pool Villas offer the best elevated views over the resort and sea at a slightly lower price point. Both categories include private pools.

faqs.question: How to get there?
faqs.answer: Da Nang International Airport receives direct international flights from Singapore, Bangkok, Kuala Lumpur, Tokyo, Seoul, and other Asian hubs. Transfer to the resort takes approximately 30 minutes. From Europe and North America, connections via Singapore, Bangkok, or Hanoi are typical.
